front.title: Post-Quantum Cryptographic Agility Profile for ACME

abstract.t: This document defines an Automated Certificate Management Environment (ACME) profile extension that enables ACME clients and servers to negotiate post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) posture per account and per order. The extension carries PQC algorithm preferences, a hybrid_required policy bit, a rotation_epoch_id, and an optional composite_chain_policy identifier.
The profile is designed for both public and private ACME deployments. It does not modify the core ACME state machine; it only adds discoverable capability metadata and per-order policy directives.

note.title: Source and Archival
note.t: Source for this draft is maintained at https://github.com/Sanc-Admin/acme-pqc-agility-profile. A citable archival version of this document is published at Zenodo upon each tagged release. Author ORCID iD: https://orcid.org/0009-0006-6395-5308. Author professional credentials: https://www.credly.com/users/brian-vicente.
Discussion of this document occurs on the IETF "acme" mailing list (acme@ietf.org). Issues and pull requests may be filed at the GitHub repository linked above.

note.title: IPR Considerations
note.t: The author has filed United States patent applications covering subject matter relevant to this document, including U.S. Provisional Patent Application No. 64/080,137 (filed 2026-06-01) and U.S. Patent Application No. 19/698,870 (filed 2026-06-05). By posting this Internet-Draft, the author submits to the IETF Trust the rights described in Section 5 of BCP 78 and BCP 79. Patent licensing terms are not yet known. Implementers and reviewers should consult the IETF Datatracker IPR disclosure page for this document for current disclosure status. The Sanctum SecOps Private Enterprise Number (PEN) root used for any OID allocations referenced in companion documents is 1.3.6.1.4.1.65953.

Patent-Pending Claim Scope: The scope of the pending patent applications referenced above includes, but is not limited to, the orchestration, observability, and tenant-isolation mechanisms described in this document insofar as they intersect the claims of the referenced applications. This Internet-Draft itself does not grant any patent license. Specific implementation parameters that would extend beyond the disclosed interface (including but not limited to scoring weights, rotation triggers, tenant carve-out parameters, and adequacy thresholds) are intentionally out of scope and are not disclosed in this Internet-Draft.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: ACME is the dominant protocol for automated certificate issuance. As deployments migrate to PQC and hybrid certificate chains, ACME implementations need a uniform way to express PQC capability and per-account policy. This document defines that uniform extension.

section.name: Terminology
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "SHOULD", "MAY" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 [RFC2119] [RFC8174] when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

section.name: Directory Metadata
section.t: An ACME server compliant with this profile MUST include a pqcAgility object in its directory metadata. The object advertises supported PQC algorithm identifiers, hybrid modes, and composite chain policies.

section.name: Order Object Extensions
section.t: An ACME client MAY include a pqcAgility member in an order object. The member carries pqc_algorithm_preferences, hybrid_required, rotation_epoch_id, and composite_chain_policy fields. The server MUST honor or reject the order based on policy.

section.name: Tenant Isolation
section.t: Multi-tenant ACME servers MUST ensure that pqcAgility directives from one account do not influence issuance decisions for other accounts. Concrete tenant-isolation implementation parameters are deployment-specific and are not disclosed in this Internet-Draft.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: PQC posture is sensitive metadata. Servers SHOULD avoid exposing aggregated tenant PQC posture without authorization. Clients SHOULD validate hybrid chains end-to-end.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: IANA is requested to create an ACME PQC Agility registry for algorithm identifiers, hybrid-mode codes, and composite chain policy identifiers.

front.title: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
Ambiguity of Uppercase vs Lowercase in RFC 2119 Key Words
